result:
I now have the full, verified call path. Here's the trace from command dispatch to the physical DB write, for `SET`:

## Call path: SET dispatch → db.c write

| # | Hop | File:Line |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `processCommand()` — looks up the command | `src/server.c:4414` (call site `4457`: `cmd = lookupCommand(c->argv, c->argc)`) |
| 2 | `lookupCommand()` → delegates to `lookupCommandLogic(server.commands, argv, argc, 0)` | `src/server.c:3611` (delegate call `3612`) |
| 3 | `processCommand()` dispatches to execution | `src/server.c:4790`: `call(c, flags)` |
| 4 | `call()` invokes the command's proc pointer | `src/server.c:3951` (dispatch at `4017`: `c->cmd->proc(c)` → resolves to `setCommand` for SET) |
| 5 | `setCommand()` — parses args, then delegates | `src/t_string.c:435` (call at `443`: `setGenericCommand(c, args.flags, c->argv[1], &(c->argv[2]), args.expire, ...)`) |
| 6 | `setGenericCommand()` — existence check | `src/t_string.c:87` (lookup at `104`: `lookupKeyWriteWithLink(c->db, key, &link)`, defined `src/db.c:380`) |
| 7 | `setGenericCommand()` — issues the actual key write | `src/t_string.c:181`: `setKeyByLink(c, c->db, key, valref, setkey_flags, &link)` |
| 8 | `setKeyByLink()` — branches on whether key exists | `src/db.c:754` |
| 9a | …if key exists → overwrite value | `src/db.c:777`: `dbSetValue(db, key, valref, *link, 1, 1, flags & SETKEY_KEEPTTL)` |
| 9b | …if key doesn't exist → add new key | `src/db.c:785`: `dbAddByLink(db, key, valref, link)` |
| 10a | `dbSetValue()` (static) — replaces value in place | `src/db.c:575`, low-level write at `657`: `kvstoreDictSetAtLink(db->keys, slot, kvNew, &link, 0)` |
| 10b | `dbAddByLink()` — thin wrapper | `src/db.c:460` (calls `dbAddInternal` at `463`) |
| 11b | `dbAddInternal()` — builds the kvobj and inserts it | `src/db.c:416`, low-level write at `425`: `kvstoreDictSetAtLink(db->keys, slot, kv, link, 1)` |

**Lowest-level write in db.c**: both paths converge on `kvstoreDictSetAtLink()` (`db.c:657` for overwrite, `db.c:425` for insert) — this is the call that actually installs the `kvobj` into `db->keys` (a `kvstore`, Redis's per-slot dict-of-dicts). The function itself is implemented in `src/kvstore.c:879`, outside db.c, so `db.c`'s own "floor" is the call site, not the dict internals.

Side note: if a TTL was given, `setGenericCommand()` also calls `setExpireByLink(c, c->db, key->ptr, milliseconds, link)` at `t_string.c:184` to write the expiry into `db->expires` — a parallel low-level write not shown above since the prompt asked specifically about the key-value write.

**Method/coverage**: `lookupCommand`/`call`/`setCommand`/`setGenericCommand`/`setKeyByLink`/`dbAddByLink`/`dbAddInternal`/`dbSetValue` chain confirmed by direct Read of each function body plus one `incomingCalls` LSP check (`setKeyByLink` ← `setGenericCommand:181`, matching the source). `outgoingCalls` wasn't supported by this clangd instance ("method not found"), so the rest of the chain was verified by reading definitions directly rather than via LSP call-hierarchy in the forward direction — all in the single compile config (`compile_commands.json` at repo root), no out-of-DB files involved.
